projects.raw_text:
Replica path and hierarchical bitline design for High speed SRAM

Jul 2021 – Dec 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Hierarchical bitline architecture was designed to reduce the load on bitline to improve the read operation of SRAM.
Replica path was designed to generate the sense amplifier enable signal in time across all process variation
A faster reset ckt was implemented to improve the access time of the SRAM

Other contributors



View all contributors
Image processing using SDSOC for  noise removal and image sharpening

Jan 2021 – May 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Show project
Guide: Dr. Sumit Darak

A median filter and a 2DFIR filter was implemented both in software and hardware(Board used was ZEDBOARD). A noisy image( salt & pepper noise) was given as an input and the output image was observed after it passed through median and 2DFIR filter. The median filter was used to eliminate salt and pepper noise and 2DFIR filter was to sharpen and smoothen the image. A speed up of 10 was achieved wrt the software implementation when both the filter function was carried out in hardware. Difference in the output image and performance when kernel size and wordlength were varied, was also reported.

Other contributors



View all contributors
Design of High-speed SRAM array

Jan 2021 – Apr 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide : Dr. Anuj Grover

A high-speed 6T and 8T(1R and 1RW) SRAM array was designed in 65nm technology. Simulation was done in eldo to calculate cell current,leakage ,SNM and bitline discharge rate for different loads. Layout was made in Calibre for both 6T and 8T and it was DRC and LVS clean. Post-layout simulation changes were marginal.

Other contributors




View all contributors
RTL to GDS flow for given functional specification

Jan 2021 – Apr 2021


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. Sneh Saurabh

RTL was designed based on the given specification in Verilog. Synthesis was done in Cadence genus for different constraints given. Static timing analysis was done in Tempus at various stages of the flow. Equivalence checking was done in conformal to compare the RTL and the synthesized netlist. DFT was done in Cadence Genus and physical design was also performed.

Other contributors



View all contributors
Design of OAI321 complex gate using Domino logic

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr.Anuj Grover

A complex Gate OAI321 was designed and simulated and a compact layout was designed. The layout was DRC and LVS clean. The pre-layout and post-layout changes were marginal. Cadence Virtuoso tool was used for designing, simulation was done in ELDO and layout was done in calibre.

Other contributors



View all contributors
Design of a differential amplifier using Recycling Folded Cascode Topology

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr.G.S.Visveswaran

A differential amplifier with Recycling Folded Cascode topology was designed in Cadence 180gpdk technology. Simulation result showed the improvement achieved in terms of GBW and Slew Rate compared to Folded Cascode.

Other contributors

View all contributors
Runtime Hardware Trojan Detection

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. Sujay Deb

Hardware performance Counter’s microarchitectural datapoints were monitored using PERF tool. Based on the code (Benchmark and Trojan infiltrated code) different datasets were generated by running a script and fed to a machine learning model for training. Trained model was used to implement the static approach and an accuracy of 80% was achieved. Later datasets were collected for different timestamps and dynamic implementation was achieved by exploiting multithreading. Accuracy of the model was found to be 71% for dynamic approach. Bellman Ford was used as a benchmark and wastage of hardware resources were targeted as trojan indication.
